description: To direct one's awareness toward something; to give focused presence. 'Theonu' names what attention does—the active turning of mind toward object.
sound_symbolism: The word focuses gently. 'The' breathes with thoughtful opening, attention beginning. 'O' opens with receptive space, making room. Then 'nu' settles with grounded presence, attention landing.
grammatical_notes: Functions as verb: 'mi theonu' (I attend). Takes object of attention. Related to mindfulness practice—conscious directing of awareness.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Right attention—the skillful directing of awareness that is foundation of practice.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Attending to others—the respect shown through genuine attention in communication.
